snapshot.method: Manually curated from public reporting as of April 26 2026. Update when new announcements are made.
snapshot.scope: Confirmed defections, suspensions, and explicit standing-down announcements since GE2024 affecting current sitting MPs only. Reinstated MPs (e.g. Oliver Ryan/Burnley, Burgon, Byrne, Long-Bailey, Hussain, McDonnell, Begum) are NOT listed here as they have rejoined the Parliamentary Labour Party.
snapshot.licence: Compilation: CC-BY 4.0 (data points are from public reporting; this aggregation is editorial).

gorton-and-denton.reason: Andrew Gwynne suspended (WhatsApp messages, Feb 2025) → resigned from Parliament 23 Jan 2026 citing ill health; by-election 26 Feb 2026 won by Green Party (Hannah Spencer). Treat as fresh open seat.

runcorn-and-helsby.reason: Mike Amesbury suspended after assault conviction; resigned. By-election 1 May 2025 won by Reform UK (Sarah Pochin). Treat as fresh open seat.

hackney-north-and-stoke-newington.reason: Diane Abbott suspended from Labour Party 17 Jul 2025 after BBC interview remarks. Senior Labour sources say unlikely to be readmitted. May stand as Independent — bypass Indep cap given strong personal vote (40+ years as MP for the seat).

coventry-south.reason: Zarah Sultana left Labour Jul 2025 to co-found new left-of-Labour party with Jeremy Corbyn. Treat as fresh open seat with high-independent eligibility.

north-east-somerset-and-hanham.reason: Dan Norris suspended Apr 2025 following arrest for sexual offences and rape. Treat as open seat.

poole.reason: Neil Duncan-Jordan whip removed for voting against welfare reforms 2025. Status unresolved.

alloa-and-grangemouth.reason: Brian Leishman whip removed for welfare-rebellion vote 2025.

north-east-hertfordshire.reason: Chris Hinchliff whip removed for welfare-rebellion vote 2025.

york-central.reason: Rachael Maskell whip removed for welfare-rebellion vote 2025.

east-wiltshire.reason: Danny Kruger defected from Conservative to Reform UK on 15 September 2025. Sitting MP at time of defection. Assume he stands again as Reform UK incumbent.

newark.reason: Robert Jenrick (former Home Secretary) defected from Conservative to Reform UK on 16 January 2026. Sitting MP at time of defection.

romford.reason: Andrew Rosindell defected from Conservative to Reform UK on 18 January 2026. Sitting MP at time of defection.

fareham-and-waterlooville.reason: Suella Braverman (former Home Secretary) defected from Conservative to Reform UK on 26 January 2026. Sitting MP at time of defection.

islington-north.reason: Jeremy Corbyn won as Independent in 2024 with 49.2% (15,977 votes). High personal vote — bypass the 8% Independent ceiling.

rochdale.reason: Paul Waugh (Lab/Co-op) won 2024; George Galloway's Workers Party + 2024 Indep dynamic still active here. Bypass Indep cap.

blackburn.reason: Adnan Hussain won as Independent 2024 (Gaza-Independent dynamic). High-personal-vote case.

leicester-south.reason: Shockat Adam won as Independent 2024 (Gaza-Independent dynamic). High-personal-vote case.

dewsbury-and-batley.reason: Iqbal Mohamed won as Independent 2024 (Gaza-Independent dynamic). High-personal-vote case.

birmingham-perry-barr.reason: Ayoub Khan won as Independent 2024 (Gaza-Independent dynamic). High-personal-vote case.
